title: Guidelines for Rebirth
wikitext:
[]: Guidelines for Rebirth             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Dec 16, 2014 7:35 pm I thought an interesting line of discussion (after reading one of Bagheera's posts in the Rebuild forum) would be on who, exactly, gets to come back after 3I. Bags states that the people born/living after 2I are fair game-- I would cut that down a little bit by postulating that only people that were alive when 3I happened would be able to go through rebirth. I feel that if you've died, you're dead. There's no coming back unless you were tanged.

[]: Re: Guidelines for Rebirth             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Dec 16, 2014 8:10 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: I feel that if you've died, you're dead. There's no coming back unless you were tanged.

[]:             Quote Post by IronEvangelion » Wed Dec 17, 2014 2:00 am Since you didn't even have to be alive when 3I happened to get instrumentalized, I'll just assume that anyone means literally anyone , or at least anyone post-2I. The unique nature of Adam's impact (which is supposed to eradicate Lilith-based life forms instead of absorbing them IIRC) leads me to believe that the souls of those who died in 2I were completely destroyed. I personally prefer to stop it at 2I myself because I really don't want to think about Shinji and Asuka being reborn into a new world where Hitler can re-materialize out of a puddle of orange goo at any time (and I'm absolutely certain he would jump at the chance).

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Dec 17, 2014 2:03 am I haven't thought about the theory of soul decay before, but it's one that appeals to me. The idea of souls decaying over time is interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Dec 17, 2014 2:42 am IronEvangelion wrote: Since you didn't even have to be alive when 3I happened to get instrumentalized, I'll just assume that anyone means literally anyone , or at least anyone post-2I. The unique nature of Adam's impact (which is supposed to eradicate Lilith-based life forms instead of absorbing them IIRC) leads me to believe that the souls of those who died in 2I were completely destroyed.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Dec 17, 2014 8:52 am 1. Lilith collected everybody ever. Whether you accept Bagheera's completely wrong, silly, and heretical "connected to people" interpretation or my own correct, intellectual, and sexually arousing fifth dimensional hyperRei interpretation this is plainly true as souls are shown to be collected pre-3I. 2. Most people ever are dead. Dying is not something easy to get over. 3. Imagining one's self in one's own heart as Rei-Lilith puts it is not a trivial matter. Some dead people are going to come back. Some living-at-the-time people are not going to come back. No information is actually provided as to what precise state the souls of humanity reside in at the end, whether they're conscious, partially merged, in a pre-Instrumentality like Shinji experienced, etc. 4. However we can conclude that returning is not trivial. Some time passes between Shinji's return (which is handed to him and not an act of power) and Asuka's triumphant resumption of physical form which left her weak and exhausted. 5. Why am I numbering these? 6. Most of what makes a person a person is physical which is why the series equates loss of corporeal form with death and why Seele is a suicide cult and Instrumentality is not a forced apotheosis but mass murder. Take that away and tremendous acts of will or technology or the patronage of the mother goddess are required. Most people in the ocean are just going to dissolve, and maybe be reborn? The series never directly addresses reincarnation but the number of souls is evidently finite. If a soul has been multiple people, which one comes back? All of them? What if through quantum insanity there is only one soul that's everybody at different times and all sex is masturbation? Then what? The more important question is this: Is the end of EoE a sunrise or a sunset?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Dec 17, 2014 10:42 am Chuckman wrote: The more important question is this: Is the end of EoE a sunrise or a sunset?

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Wed Dec 17, 2014 10:48 am We already know people who can imagine themselves in their own heart can return, the question isn't "who is capable?" But "who is given the chance to?" There is definitely a limited number of souls (as I explained in the other thread), so reincarnation must be at play. How I'd explain it is this: Kaji had died before Third Impact, so his soul is now separate from his body, and in line for being reincarnated. While the soul is separate it remains an essence of Kaji, but if it becomes the core of another being through reincarnation then it is "rewritten", erasing Kaji from all existence, and his opportunity to reconstitute after Third Impact is lost. Asuka having died moments before means that her soul is still her core or essence, and not yet belonging to another identity. The question then of whether a character can come back or not is how soon before Third Impact they had died and how often new life is in need of a soul, so that the soul had been collected whilst still belonging to them. Personally I'd consider everyone who died on the day to be eligible, but not people who died as early as Kaji. Though, that's just preference of what seems likely to me. Also, I wouldn't consider the Evangelions to have "rewritten" souls, they're still the souls of Yui etcetera, as this isn't reincarnation but being moving from one place to another.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Dec 17, 2014 7:23 pm Monk Ed wrote: FWIW, NGE2 suggests that it can happen, although Kaworu was not exactly talking in ordinary circumstances.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Dec 17, 2014 8:17 pm So souls are implied to be more or less recycled in Kaworu's NGE2 Good End (he clears his Scenario getting that ending), I think Lilith in Rei's Scenario might imply something about soul recycling too, though I can't remember the details. I tend to think the cut-off point for folks returning from the LCL Sea is somewhere around Kaji's death, myself (since he's in both EoTV and EoE Instrumentality). I just think if the people at 2I's epicenter were in Instrumentality too, there would have been some indication somewhere (in-series, supplementary materials, etc). Actually, do any of the supplementary materials like Chronicle say anything interesting about Instrumentality, or nothing terribly different from the NGE2 PSP game?

[]:             Quote Post by IronEvangelion » Thu Dec 18, 2014 1:49 am Reichu wrote: Second Impact destroyed the physical forms of Lilith-based life, but not necessarily the spiritual aspect. Had Adam lived long enough, she could have hypothetically harvested at least the Lilin souls and reappropriated them for new Angels. (NGE2 establishes that Lilin can be reborn through Impact as Angels.)

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Dec 18, 2014 1:51 am I have NGE2 on my PSP but can't play it cuz I can't understand Japanese. I bought it thinking "one day" and that day still hasn't come and I haven't really done anything to make it come.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Dec 18, 2014 2:20 pm Since I don't have the hacking tools to play my PS2 copy (sort of on my computer), I just play my PSP copy every so often. I find the text alongside the spoken Japanese makes it click more for me compared to just text or just audio. I still think it's the best Eva game, but maybe most wouldn't like the world simulator aspect?
